slides.title: Understanding Transformers
slides.subtitle: A Comprehensive Guide to Polynomial Feature Transformation

slides.title: Introduction to Transformers
slides.content: Transformers are used to alter data representations. This can include converting linear features into polynomial features to improve model performance.

slides.title: Polynomial Feature Transformation
slides.content: By transforming the data into polynomial features, we can capture nonlinear relationships in the data that simple linear models might miss.

slides.title: Implementation in Python
slides.content: Using Python's sklearn library, we can easily apply polynomial transformations. This involves creating a PolynomialFeatures object and fitting it to our data.

slides.title: Code Example
slides.content: Here's a Python code snippet to demonstrate polynomial transformation and comparing it with linear regression.

visual_assets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.preprocessing import PolynomialFeatures
quadratic_featurizer = PolynomialFeatures(degree=2)
X_train_quadratic = quadratic_featurizer.fit_transform(X_train)

slides.title: Model Comparison
slides.content: The chart below compares predictions of a linear model versus a polynomial model, highlighting the improved fit of the latter.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: Transformers, particularly polynomial transformers, are powerful tools for enhancing model predictions by capturing complex patterns in the data.
